mechanism_narrative: A persistently low and stable federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary conditions, reducing the cost of capital and supporting higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS has been low since the 2008 crisis and is currently 0.65% as of 2017-01-01.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat on average during periods of low FEDFUNDS, or if the Rank IC is consistently below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate, specifically a rise of 0.3 percentage points or more over three months, signals an imminent economic slowdown or recession, leading to a sharp decline in corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 4.8% in 2017-01-01, near historical lows, suggesting sensitivity to any upward movement.
failure_condition: If, following a trigger, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 40% of the time, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth combined with contained inflation suggests strong consumer demand without significant cost pressures. This environment supports corporate revenue and profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate strong earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 472142.0 in 2017-01-01, CPIAUCSL was 244.158, indicating moderate growth and inflation.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods where both conditions are met, or if the interaction term shows no significant predictive power (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an economic expansion indicates increasing capacity utilization and strong business investment, leading to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. However, this effect reverses in a contraction.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 104.5548 in 2017-01-01, showing recent growth.
failure_condition: If the signal fails to predict positive returns during expansionary periods, or if the direction of returns is positive even during periods of contraction when INDPRO is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts suggests weakening consumer and business confidence, impacting future construction activity and related sectors. This indicates a broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1226.0 in 2016-12-01, indicating a healthy level, so a sharp drop would be significant.
failure_condition: If a sustained decline in HOUST is followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns more than 50% of the time, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong and consistent growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenues. This positive economic momentum translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the short term of 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 145554.0 in 2017-01-01, suggesting a strong labor market.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or flat on average during periods of strong PAYEMS growth, or if the Rank IC is consistently below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unexpectedly high and accelerating inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, potentially forcing the central bank to tighten monetary policy. This creates uncertainty and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 244.158 in 2017-01-01, indicating moderate inflation, so a spike would be a clear signal.
failure_condition: If periods of high and accelerating CPIAUCSL are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns more than 45% of the time, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong and sustained growth in real GDP indicates a robust and expanding economy, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence. This fundamental strength drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 16804.8 in 2016-10-01, showing a healthy economy.
failure_condition: If periods of strong GDPC1 growth are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns more than 35% of the time, or if the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).
